summary.intent: Fix three Codex review findings on PR #710 for the GridList component: preserve range-selection anchor for repeated Shift+Arrow, filter structural collection keys from initial/controlled selections, and remove invalid aria-selected from non-composite listitem rows.
summary.outcome: All three fixes were implemented with regression tests, the snapshot was updated, spec wording was corrected, and commit 243fd006 (plus a follow-up) was pushed to PR #710 with all Codex threads replied to and resolved.

repo: GridList uses a range_anchor_key on context to preserve the original anchor across repeated Shift+Arrow presses; without it the keyboard helper cannot infer the starting anchor after the first range update.
Structural collection keys (separator, section, header) must be excluded from both selection filtering and focus filtering to maintain a consistent invariant across init, prop sync, and user events.
Non-composite listitem rows must not emit aria-selected; selection state is conveyed via data attributes (data-selected) instead.
The project uses cargo insta for snapshot testing; stale .snap.new files left from failed runs must be manually removed from the git index before committing.
cargo xcov is the local coverage gate and rebuilds with instrumentation — it is the slowest local check in the CI pipeline.
cargo xtask spec validate and cargo xtask spec compile-snippets are required spec tooling checks before committing spec changes.
The project uses @codex review comment triggers on GitHub issues to initiate automated code review passes.

code.finding: Context initialization surface where range_anchor_key field was added.
Transition/connect behavior patched to store anchor on SelectRange and clear it on non-range focus moves.
Row attrs logic patched so non-composite rows only emit data-selected, not aria-selected.
Selection helpers updated: filtering now requires a focusable value node, and helpers clear or preserve anchors deliberately.
Repeated Shift+Arrow regression test inserted before the existing range-edge test; second range must extend from the original anchor.
Snapshot intentionally updated to reflect removal of aria-selected from non-composite row output.
Spec wording updated to document that non-composite listitems expose selection via data attributes, not aria-selected.

workflow: Patches that span too many changed regions fail to apply cleanly; splitting into smaller, targeted edits against exact current line ranges is more reliable.
After cargo insta accept, always rerun the focused test suite to confirm the accepted snapshot is the only delta.
When adding test helpers in the same test function, shadowing the helper name causes compile errors; rename intermediate bindings to avoid shadowing.
The Codex review loop pattern: push → reply to threads → resolve threads → post @codex review trigger → poll reactions and thread state → fix new findings → repeat.

CI matrix includes clippy, adapter parity, integration, error coverage, formatting, snapshot, mutual-exclusion, DOM browser, i18n browser, core flags, coverage, unit tests, Leptos feature flags, Dioxus feature flags, and release verification — polling in ~2 minute intervals is appropriate.

friction: Large multi-region patch failed to apply cleanly and had to be split into multiple smaller apply_patch calls.
Stale .snap.new file left from a failed snapshot run was staged in git and had to be manually removed with git restore --staged before committing.
Non-composite data-selected assertion was initially placed in the wrong test scenario (one that focuses gamma but doesn't select it), requiring a correction pass.
Helper name shadowing in a test function caused a compile error that required a targeted rename fix.
Long CI matrix with many parallel jobs meant multiple polling rounds (10+ iterations at ~2 minute intervals) before all checks completed.
Codex review posted three new finding batches across multiple polling cycles, requiring repeated fix/push/resolve/retrigger loops.
open_items: Proptest state machine tests for data_display are configured as ignored for the nightly extended job — confirm they are exercised in the full nightly run.

The focus-filtering invariant (structural keys cannot remain focused) was tightened alongside the selection fix — verify this doesn't regress any focus-restoration edge cases on component unmount/remount.

Coverage threshold for ars-components was described as passing at the branch threshold — monitor whether new code paths added by these fixes require additional coverage to stay above the threshold long-term.
